notes: AMD budget desktop CPU brand, introduced July 2004 to replace Athlon XP in low-end segment; based on Athlon/Thoroughbred/Palermo cores; direct competitor to Intel Celeron
AMD 32-bit desktop CPU brand, used K7 Palomino/Thoroughbred/Barton cores; succeeded by Athlon 64 (2003) and Sempron low-end (2004)
AMD first desktop 64-bit CPU (Sep 2003, K8 microarchitecture); brought AMD64 ISA to consumer desktops ahead of Intel EM64T
Intel budget desktop/laptop CPU brand; direct competitor to AMD Sempron; long-running brand through Pentium Gold/Silver era
Locally-assembled unbranded PCs dominant in emerging markets through mid-2000s; displaced by Chinese OEM branded PCs and tablet/smartphone shift post-2010
